commands.review_summary:
The PR removes `saveMediaSource` write-time pruning, maps non-recursive media cleanup to root-only pruning, adds a regression test, and updates `media.ttlHours` help text.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +22. Total +36 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and supplied real-host screenshots: current shipped code calls `saveMediaSource` cleanup with a two-minute TTL, and fs-safe descends into first-level media directories when `maxDepth: 1`. I did not run a local live reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer retention-policy decision, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(media): stop pruning media on write; let the configured timer do it
target.body:
## Summary `saveMediaSource` ran an inline `cleanOldMedia(DEFAULT_TTL_MS, { recursive: false })` on every call. Two problems compounded: 1. `DEFAULT_TTL_MS` is **2 minutes**, hardcoded — it ignores the configurable `media.ttlHours` retention. 2. `cleanOldMedia` mapped `{ recursive: false }` to `pruneExpired({ maxDepth: 1, … })`. The `@openclaw/fs-safe` prune walker keys descent off `maxDepth` whenever it is set (`shouldDescend = maxDepth !== undefined ? depth < maxDepth : recursive`), so `recursive: false` still descended one level — into `media/inbound/`, `media/outbound/`, `media/tool-image-generation/`, etc. Net effect: preparing _any_ outgoing attachment silently mass-deleted unrelated attachments and AI-generated images older than 2 minutes across all first-level `media/` subdirectories, regardless of `media.ttlHours`. `media/` is canonical storage now (not the temporary Twilio scratch dir it was when this was added in `948ff7f`), so this is destructive legacy behavior. `pruneExpired` swallows per-file errors, so the deletions were unlogged. ## Fix Remove the inline cleanup from `saveMediaSource`. Media cleanup is now owned solely by the gateway maintenance timer (`startGatewayMaintenanceTimers`), which runs a full-tree recursive sweep using the operator-configured `media.ttlHours`. With `media.ttlHours` unset (default), no automatic cleanup runs — cleanup is opt-in. recursive: false now resolves to depth 0 (root-only) instead of wrongly descending one level. ## Behavior change - Writing media (`saveMediaSource` / `saveMediaBuffer` / `saveMediaStream`) never prunes. - Media is only pruned when `media.ttlHours` is set, via the maintenance timer. - `media.ttlHours` unset → no automatic media cleanup (help text updated accordingly). ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Preparing _any_ outgoing attachment silently mass-deleted unrelated attachments and AI-generated images older than 2 minutes across all first-level `media/` subdirectories, regardless of `media.ttlHours`. - Real environment tested: v2026.6.1 / Fixed branch - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - Ensure /media/inbound has a file >2 minutes old - Open web chat - Attach image - Request image generation - Check /media/inbound to see if file is deleted - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Screenshot of files now preserved - Observed result after fix: Files in /media are no longer deleted by attaching a file in web chat - What was not tested: Scheduled pruning (ttlHours) - Proof limitations or environment constraints: None - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Screenshot of files missing after attaching a file in web chat ## Verification - The `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.3.0` `pruneExpiredStoreEntries` descent logic was ported into a standalone Node reproduction and run against real temp directories, confirming the old `{ recursive: false }` deleted `media/inbound/` (the reported bug) and that `cleanOldMedia(ttlMs)` removes root-level and first-level expired files and does not follow symlinked directories. - The gateway timer's effective `pruneExpired` arguments are unchanged (`recursive: true, pruneEmptyDirs: true`, `maxDepth` undefined), so the recursive cleanup tests retain their original behavior. - Ran the related test suites locally — `pnpm test src/media/store.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts` — and all tests pass. Type-check (`pnpm tsgo`) is clean. - Built a Docker image from fix branch and installed it on a host. Confirmed files are no longer deleted when sending file attachment in web chat. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No What is the highest-risk area? Media file cleanup How is that risk mitigated? Ran code to confirm file cleanup works as expected. ## Current review state What is the next action? What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.
